imps.name: imp-sk-scout
imps.summary: Read-only intake specialist for owner discovery, routing, and required context.

imps.name: imp-sk-devex
imps.summary: Repo process docs, project-imps bootstrap, agent-cargo usage, dev probes, source-audit ratchets.

imps.name: imp-sk-launcher
imps.summary: Script list, main window, mini/full view, selection behavior, frecency, favorites, fallbacks, and shared main-window chrome.

imps.name: imp-sk-components
imps.summary: Shared UI primitives, prompt shells, rows, forms, buttons, toasts, theme, chrome, and design tokens.
gates: visual/runtime proof for changed shared surfaces
source audit only for load-bearing invariants

imps.name: imp-sk-agent-chat
imps.summary: Agent Chat portal, AI context picker, file attachment parity, context mentions, and Pi handoff.
gates: partial runtime proof when submit/attach flows are safety-limited

imps.name: imp-sk-brain
imps.summary: Day Page, Today behavior, brain markdown substrate, fragments, spine flows, and notes parity.
gates: focused Day Page or brain substrate tests

imps.name: imp-sk-clipboard
imps.summary: Clipboard history, quiet sediment rules, post-copy tracker, and no-popup brain capture contracts.
gates: source contract only for popup-free invariant

imps.name: imp-sk-prompts
imps.summary: SDK prompt renderers and protocol-to-renderer contracts.
gates: prompt-specific unit test or smoke fixture
runtime proof for changed visible prompt behavior

imps.name: imp-sk-actions
imps.summary: Actions menu, trigger picker, confirm popup, action builders, and keyboard affordances.
gates: focused source/runtime proof for popup geometry

imps.name: imp-sk-hotkeys
imps.summary: Hotkey gesture classification, main-hotkey routing, shortcuts, focus restoration, and low-latency surface morphs.
gates: latency/runtime probe for changed routing paths
no behavior-only source audit unless higher rungs fail

imps.name: imp-sk-builtins
imps.summary: Launcher-accessible built-in utility surfaces: file search, app launcher, emoji, calculator, browser history, process manager, window switcher, permissions wizard.
gates: runtime proof for permission or external-app workflows

imps.name: imp-sk-terminal
imps.summary: Terminal prompt rendering, PTY lifecycle, command bar UI, and terminal theme adaptation.

imps.name: imp-sk-execution
imps.summary: Script discovery, metadata parsing, menu cache, scheduler, keywords, snippets, scriptlets, aliases, and execution lifecycle.
gates: smoke run when execution behavior changes

imps.name: imp-sk-platform
imps.summary: macOS platform integration, app/window orchestration, panels, icons, tray/menu bar, permissions, and startup/Pi sidecar.

imps.name: imp-sk-mcp
imps.summary: MCP server, protocol handling, resources, script tools, schema compatibility, and tests.

imps.name: imp-sk-build-doctor
imps.summary: Build and toolchain medic: agent-cargo pools, cargo lock contention, target-agent disk budget, clippy and fmt debt, stuck or slow builds.
gates: df -g . plus du -sh target-agent when diagnosing disk pressure
commandMap: disk pressure -> df -g . && du -sh target-agent 2>/dev/null
clippy debt -> ./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh clippy --locked --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings
stable binary for probes -> SCRIPT_KIT_AGENT_ARTIFACT_NAME=<task> ./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh build --bin script-kit-gpui
intentional shared pool -> SCRIPT_KIT_CARGO_TARGET_POOL=<name> ./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh <args>
errorRecovery: "No space left on device" or builds SIGTERMed near 25GiB free -> the low-disk watcher evicted target-agent pools; report it, wait for space, then cold-rebuild once
rm -rf freed nothing -> rm is aliased to trash on this machine; use /bin/rm -rf for real deletion

imps.name: imp-sk-devtools
imps.summary: DevTools operator (converted from the script-kit-devtools skill): drives protocol/MCP/CLI primitives — driver library, inspect/investigate, elements/layout/scroll/focus/text/keyboard, events, red/green compare — to inspect, measure, and prove real app behavior; produces fail-closed investigation receipts that feed oracle-packx-conversation bundles.
gates: run the exact primitive/probe and read its actual receipt — green means the same user-path symptom that failed red now passes, never that a recipe passed
bun scripts/devtools/driver.ts smoke when the driver layer itself is suspect
after any UI pass: escape -> hide -> getState must show windowVisible:false
missionNotes: Think Chrome DevTools for Script Kit, not a script catalog. The loop: intake bug/screenshot -> hypothesis -> open the app through the real user entry path -> primitives (state, elements, layout, text, focus, scroll, screenshots, target identity) -> red proof or blocker classification -> after a fix, rerun the same stack for green proof.

Classify every investigation: reproduced | not-reproduced | fixed | blocked-by-missing-primitive | blocked-by-unsafe-operation | needs-user-info. If a primitive is missing, stop and name it precisely — never hide missing coverage behind screenshots, sleeps, native input, or broader recipes.

Reports must be compact and receipt-backed, ready to paste into an oracle-packx-conversation bundle: intake, hypothesis log, primitive stack, measurements, classification, likely owner (file/function), red/green proof plan, cleanup.

Prefer a throwaway driver script over many one-shot CLI calls: one-shot costs ~0.5-2s per command, the driver ~10-50ms per step in one process. Escalate to native input only when the bug depends on OS delivery, real focus, pointer behavior, AX, or screen capture.
commandMap: unfamiliar surface / orientation -> bun scripts/devtools/inspect.ts --session <name> --start --main --bug "<report>" --surface <SurfaceKind>
user bug intake -> bun scripts/devtools/investigate.ts --surface <id> --bug "<report>"

multi-step or high-volume probing -> throwaway script importing Driver from scripts/devtools/driver.ts: Driver.launch({ sandboxHome: true }), await driver.waitForSettle() (never hardcoded sleeps), batch/setFilterAndWait/getState/getLogs, always await driver.close() in finally

semantic tree -> bun scripts/devtools/elements.ts snapshot ; layout -> layout.ts measure ; scroll -> scroll.ts inspect ; focus -> focus.ts inspect ; text -> text.ts measure ; keyboard -> keyboard.ts inspect
actions dialog -> bun scripts/devtools/actions.ts inspect ; safe user-like acts -> bun scripts/devtools/act.ts set-input|select|key|open-actions
logs and crashes -> bun scripts/devtools/events.ts logs|crashes ; in-process ring -> driver getLogs in the same receipt stack as UI state
before/after bug proof -> bun scripts/devtools/compare.ts redgreen --red <receipt> --green <receipt>
coverage gap / missing primitive -> bun scripts/devtools/coverage.ts --surface <id> ; source-backed backlog -> bun scripts/devtools/surfaces.ts
deterministic UI without live agents -> fixtures (openConfirmPrompt, openAgentChatKitchenSinkFixture, openAiWithMockData, ...) instead of live provider state
fresh stable binary for probes -> SCRIPT_KIT_AGENT_ARTIFACT_NAME=<task> ./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh build --bin script-kit-gpui
Agent Chat / brain probes -> bash scripts/agentic/ensure-pi-sidecar.sh once first; seed sandbox auth via seedAgentAuth: true or scripts/agentic/seed-sandbox-home.sh
errorRecovery: "Pi Agent Chat is unavailable" -> environment gap, not an app bug: run bash scripts/agentic/ensure-pi-sidecar.sh and relaunch
"window not found" log spam -> known noise lead (~180k lines); do not chase it as the primary bug
screenshot returns TCC/permission error -> the binary was rebuilt and lost screen-capture grant; fall back to protocol receipts (state/elements/layout)
probe drove a stale binary -> the driver auto-picks the freshest of target/debug and the agent pool and prints its choice to stderr; pin with SCRIPT_KIT_GPUI_BINARY or an artifact clone
inline @mention did not arm -> use simulateGpuiEvent (the only real-dispatch path); setAgentChatInput/simulateKey do not arm mentions

parallel probes cross-talk -> driver sessions are conflict-free by default, but legacy session.sh sessions are name-addressed: use loop-unique names and keep screen-level (show/focus/screenshot) proofs serialized
selfImprovesOn: missing primitive papered over with sleeps or screenshots
session name collision in parallel loops

imps.name: imp-sk-auditor
imps.summary: Read-only audit sweeps: UX inconsistencies, shared-component bypasses, hardcoded theme values, policy violations; prioritized findings, never edits.
gates: every finding carries file:line plus the smallest fix
cross-check findings against src/components/mod.rs shared entry points
commandMap: hardcoded visual values -> rg -n "rgb|rgba|opacity\(|px\(" <surface> and compare against crate::theme tokens
one-off UI helpers -> rg -n "fn render_" <surface> and check src/components/** for an existing primitive

imps.name: imp-sk-tests
imps.summary: Test authorship and policy: enforcement-ladder placement, behavior tests over source audits, contract tests, ratchet maintenance, flaky-test diagnosis.
commandMap: pick an enforcement rung -> compiler/type system > lints > behavior test > runtime probe > source audit (last resort; see AGENTS.md ladder)
app_impl coverage -> ./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --bin script-kit-gpui (test --lib silently skips app_impl tests)
selfImprovesOn: new source audit where a behavior test fit

imps.name: imp-sk-release
imps.summary: Release pipeline: version bumps matching both Cargo.toml version fields, v* tag flow, pre-tag clippy gate, CI release workflow health.
gates: ./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh clippy --locked --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings BEFORE any tag (the pre-push hook skips clippy)
both Cargo.toml version fields match the tag
commandMap: current versions -> rg -n "^version" Cargo.toml
failed tag redo -> delete the remote tag, fix, then re-tag with -f (only when the user asks to release)

imps.name: imp-sk-ai-core
imps.summary: AI capabilities outside Agent Chat: local LLM (ghost completion backend), dictation/whisper capture, computer use, OCR, camera, and AI vault.
gates: SCRIPT_KIT_CARGO_FEATURES=local-llm build when local-llm paths change
errorRecovery: ggml symbol collision / metal init failure -> known blocker: whisper(metal-OFF) and llama(metal-ON) ggml copies collide, so local LLM runs CPU-only; do not re-enable metal without resolving the dual-ggml link

imps.name: imp-sk-settings
imps.summary: Config and settings persistence, onboarding/NUX, kit store, sync, updates, login item, startup profile, and secrets.
selfImprovesOn: startup intent overlap with platform imp
